steps.title: Substitute x = 0 into the equation
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, which occurs when $x = 0$. Substituting into the given equation:
$$\frac{3(0)}{7}=-\frac{5y}{9}+21$$
$$0=-\frac{5y}{9}+21$$

steps.title: Solve for y
steps.content:
Add $\frac{5y}{9}$ to both sides:
$$\frac{5y}{9}=21$$
Multiply both sides by 9:
$$5y = 189$$
Divide by 5:
$$y = \frac{189}{5}$$
